SHMI Diversity Sub‑index

Source: vignettes/articles/Diversity.Rmd
Diversity.Rmd

Rotation‑Scale, Entropy‑Based Hill Diversity

Overview

The SHMI Diversity sub‑index quantifies the diversity of crops grown across an entire rotation, accounting for both species identity and time in the field. Diversity is computed using entropy‑based Hill numbers, which generalize richness, Shannon entropy, and Simpson entropy into a unified framework.

The algorithm expands crop mixtures, sums plant‑days across all years of the rotation, computes species proportions, and applies the selected Hill‑number order (default: Simpson entropy, q=2q = 2).
The final score is scaled to 0–100 using a user‑specified theoretical maximum (default: max_div = 8).

1. Daily Plant‑Days

Daily crop presence is summarized for each management unit ii, crop sequence number, crop name, and year:

daysi,j,y=∑dcrop_presenti,j,y,d \text{days}_{i,j,y} = \sum_{d} \text{crop_present}_{i,j,y,d}

where:

  • ii = management unit
  • jj = crop or mixture
  • yy = year
  • dd = day

Records with missing crop sequence numbers are excluded.

2. Mixture Expansion

Crop mixtures are expanded into individual species:

  • Mixtures like "A + B" become species A and B
  • Placeholder mixtures like "3-species" become
    species1,species2,species3\text{species}_1, \text{species}_2, \text{species}_3

After expansion, each species inherits the plant‑days of the mixture.

Fallow is treated as zero diversity:

days=0if species = "fallow" \text{days} = 0 \quad \text{if species = "fallow"}

3. Rotation‑Scale Plant‑Days

Plant‑days are summed across all years of the rotation:

daysi,s=∑ydaysi,s,y \text{days}_{i,s} = \sum_{y} \text{days}_{i,s,y}

Species proportions are then computed:

pi,s=daysi,s∑s′daysi,s′ p_{i,s} = \frac{\text{days}_{i,s}}{\sum_{s'} \text{days}_{i,s'}}

4. Entropy‑Based Hill Diversity

The diversity metric DD depends on the Hill‑number order qq:

Richness (q = 0)

D=∑I(ps>0) D = \sum I(p_s > 0)

Shannon entropy (q = 1)

D=−∑pslogps D = -\sum p_s \log p_s

Simpson entropy (q = 2, default)

D=−log(∑ps2) D = -\log \left( \sum p_s^2 \right)

The result is capped at the theoretical maximum:

D←min(D,max_div) D \leftarrow \min(D, \text{max_div})

5. Scaling to 0–100

Richness is scaled linearly:

Diversityraw=Dmax_div \text{Diversity}_{\text{raw}} = \frac{D}{\text{max_div}}

Entropy‑based metrics are scaled by the log of the maximum:

Diversityraw=Dlog(max_div) \text{Diversity}_{\text{raw}} = \frac{D}{\log(\text{max_div})}

Final score:

Diversity=100×min(Diversityraw,1) \text{Diversity} = 100 \times \min(\text{Diversity}_{\text{raw}}, 1)

Interpretation

  • 0 → Monoculture rotation (or fallow‑only)
  • 100 → Maximum theoretical diversity (e.g., 8 equally represented species)
  • Entropy‑based metrics reward both richness and evenness
  • Mixture crops contribute proportionally to diversity

Output

A data frame with one row per management unit:

MGT_combo Diversity
… 0–100

On this page
